title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers python, tcl, tkinter, threads, and the event loop into a changed single-root event-loop lifecycle contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of single-root event-loop lifecycle contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.
Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Python Tcl Tk Layers
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 9579 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Runtime Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-1ssklg" that uses case 5270 to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Runtime Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Runtime Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Runtime Evidence, keep Runtime Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-1ssklg
# Competency: maze-runtime-evidence.
# Case 5270: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_runtime_evidence_1ssklg(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ssklg = "1ssklg-glkss1-5270"
    assert evidence_variant_1ssklg
    evidence_axes_1ssklg = (
        "grid1ssklg",
        "topology5270",
        "searchglkss1",
        "event1ssklg5270",
        "access52701ssklg",
        "recoveryglkss15270",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ssklg)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"
